desc:
Bug 1934370 - Fix run-clang-tidy invocation. r=firefox-build-system-reviewers,ahochheiden

The run-clang-tidy script in clang 19 changed such that it always
normalizes paths from the database. Before that change, we were lucky
that it didn't because the paths in the database are absolute, and it
skipped calling `os.path.abspath` in that case.

On Windows, `os.path.abspath` implies slash normalization.

What that means is that the files we pass on the command line then don't
match what's in the database anymore, because we pass paths with forward
slashes, and the normalization of the database paths makes them use
backslashes.

So we need to normalize the paths we pass to clang-tidy.

But what run-clang-tidy really expects is regexps, so backslashes also
need to be escaped.

Differential Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D231189

lines.line: // Bug 1908010 - This overwrites the design system color because of a
// know transparency issue with color-mix syntax when a wallpaper is set
// Contrast fix for users who have wallpapers set
// Necessary for when navigating by a keyboard, having the context
// menu open should display the "…" button. This style is a clone
// Contrast fix for users who have wallpapers set
// Some common styles for all icons (rich and default) in top sites
transition-property: background-size, bottom, inset-inline-end, height, width;
// We want all search shortcuts to have a white background in case they have transparency.
// This animation is derived from Firefox's tab loading animation
// prevent text selection of keyword label when clicking to select
// used for tooltips below form element

desc:
Bug 1934370 - Fix run-clang-tidy invocation. r=firefox-build-system-reviewers,ahochheiden

The run-clang-tidy script in clang 19 changed such that it always
normalizes paths from the database. Before that change, we were lucky
that it didn't because the paths in the database are absolute, and it
skipped calling `os.path.abspath` in that case.

On Windows, `os.path.abspath` implies slash normalization.

What that means is that the files we pass on the command line then don't
match what's in the database anymore, because we pass paths with forward
slashes, and the normalization of the database paths makes them use
backslashes.

So we need to normalize the paths we pass to clang-tidy.

But what run-clang-tidy really expects is regexps, so backslashes also
need to be escaped.

Differential Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D231189
